PACKAGES.title: Kerala Culinary Odyssey
PACKAGES.subtitle: Half-day cooking demonstration · 11am to 3pm
PACKAGES.blurb: Four hours in our spice garden and heritage kitchen — a demonstration, a stroll, a banana-leaf lunch.
PACKAGES.longBlurb: If you have an afternoon to spare and a curiosity about Kerala’s plate, this is the gentlest way in. You’ll wander our spice garden with one of us, watch two classic dishes come together in the heritage kitchen, then sit down to eat what we’ve cooked, on banana leaf, at the long table.
highlights: Spice-garden stroll — pepper, nutmeg, cardamom in their canopies
Two-dish cooking demonstration in our heritage kitchen
Recipes sent to your inbox or WhatsApp afterwards

itinerary.title: Arrive
itinerary.body: Welcome with a herbal infusion and a quiet introduction to the farm.

itinerary.title: Spice garden walk
itinerary.body: A 45-minute wander through the bio-organic spice garden. Pepper vines, cardamom, nutmeg, vanilla, cocoa.

itinerary.title: Cooking demonstration
itinerary.body: Two authentic Kerala vegetarian dishes in our heritage kitchen. Watch, photograph, or join in lightly.

itinerary.title: Banana-leaf lunch
itinerary.body: Sit down to eat what we’ve cooked, in the traditional way.

itinerary.title: Depart
itinerary.body: Leave with recipes sent to your inbox and a small jar of our garam masala.

PACKAGES.notes: Per guest, includes lunch and bottled water. Valid until May 31, 2027.

PACKAGES.title: Hands-On Cooking & Spice Farm Day
PACKAGES.subtitle: Full-day workshop · 10am to 4pm
PACKAGES.blurb: A whole day on the farm. Garden walk, pineapple market, truck-art yard, an auto-rickshaw village ride, and four hours in the kitchen.
PACKAGES.longBlurb: Our most popular day for visitors passing through Kerala. The morning is for looking — the garden, the pineapple market in town, a local artist who paints lorries, an auto-rickshaw ride through villages most travellers never see. The afternoon is for cooking, with your hands, four to six vegetarian dishes, and a long lunch.
highlights: Visit to a working pineapple market
Meet a local artist who paints Kerala’s lorries

itinerary.title: Arrival
itinerary.body: Settle into the kitchen, meet Madhu and the team.

itinerary.title: Farm walk
itinerary.body: Permaculture principles, forest gardening, and the spices in their canopies.

itinerary.title: Out and about
itinerary.body: Pineapple market, lorry-art yard, auto-rickshaw through the villages.

itinerary.title: Hands-on cooking
itinerary.body: Four to six vegetarian dishes. Stand at the chopping block, learn the order of things, taste as you go.

itinerary.title: Lunch
itinerary.body: Eat what you’ve cooked, with the group.

itinerary.title: Depart
itinerary.body: Back to your hotel, or onward to wherever you’re going next.

PACKAGES.notes: Per guest, includes food and bottled water. 100% vegetarian.

PACKAGES.title: Curry Lovers’ Retreat
PACKAGES.subtitle: 2 nights · 3 days · the short way in
PACKAGES.blurb: A weekend in the kitchen — six to eight tailored recipes, a pottery workshop, an ancient church, and a slow ferry on the Muvattupuzha.
PACKAGES.longBlurb: Our shortest residential program, for guests with a long weekend and a serious appetite. You’ll cook six to eight dishes over two intensive sessions, walk the spice garden, see a few of our favourite places in the midlands, and sit down to plenty of long meals.
highlights: 6–8 tailored recipes across two hands-on sessions
Visit to a traditional potter and an ancient Syrian Christian church
Punting boat ride on the Muvattupuzha River

itinerary.title: Arrival & first kitchen
itinerary.body: Settle into your bungalow. Light lunch. Spice-garden tour. A spice presentation, then your first hands-on session, ending with dinner.

itinerary.title: Midlands & masterclass
itinerary.body: Morning visits to local temples and markets. Lunch at a neighbourhood restaurant. Afternoon three-hour cooking masterclass at the long kitchen.

itinerary.title: Farewell breakfast
itinerary.body: A last breakfast at the long table, recipes packed, and onward.

PACKAGES.notes: All-inclusive: AC bungalow, all meals, cooking sessions, bottled water, local excursions. Excludes taxi transfers.
PACKAGES.flexibleStart: Start any day of the week except Saturday.

PACKAGES.title: Touch, Taste & Relish
PACKAGES.subtitle: 3 nights · 4 days · the popular one
PACKAGES.blurb: Hands in the kitchen on three days, balanced with a river ferry, a handloom weaver, and our bean-to-bar chocolate demo.
PACKAGES.longBlurb: If you can afford a few more days, this is the one we recommend most. The cooking sessions are properly long and the rest days have proper rests in them. You’ll see handloom weaving, watch our chocolate being made, and ride the local ferry the way the locals ride it.
highlights: Truck-art yard, handloom weaving, rubber processing, coconut-oil making
Pineapple market, farmers’ auction, artisan pottery

itinerary.title: Orientation
itinerary.body: Arrive for a light lunch and orientation to the spice world. Culinary demonstrations and your first hands-on session.

itinerary.title: Culture & craft
itinerary.body: Morning tour of handloom weaving, rubber processing, and bean-to-bar chocolate. Afternoon cooking session on regional specialties.

itinerary.title: Markets & masterclass
itinerary.body: River ferry ride, pineapple and vegetable markets. Final cooking session. Celebratory farewell dinner.

itinerary.title: Farewell
itinerary.body: A final traditional breakfast and onward.

PACKAGES.notes: All-inclusive AC accommodation, all meals, 2–3 hour sessions, bottled water, all itinerary excursions.

PACKAGES.title: Kerala Cooking Residency
PACKAGES.subtitle: 4 nights · 5 days · the classic
PACKAGES.blurb: Our flagship residency. Four full days, the techniques behind the recipes, tea plantations and spice farms in the afternoons.
PACKAGES.longBlurb: The classic Pimenta cooking week. Long enough that we can teach you the ‘why’ behind a dish, not just the ‘how’, and short enough that you can still fit it inside an Indian holiday. You’ll cook every day, see tea country, meet our spice neighbours, and ride the river.
highlights: Minimum 4 intensive hands-on sessions (≈2 hrs each)
Mastery of iconic Kerala vegetarian dishes
Vibrant local markets, places of worship, Muvattupuzha boat trip
Day in the tea country and on a working spice farm

itinerary.title: Welcome & spice revelation
itinerary.body: Light lunch. Kerala’s culinary history. Introductory cooking session focused on the significance of spices.

itinerary.title: Market & river
itinerary.body: Hands-on session on iconic dishes. Guided tour of local markets and places of worship. A tranquil boat trip on the Muvattupuzha.

itinerary.title: Industry & artistry
itinerary.body: More hands-on cooking. The local food industry. Visit to a renowned lorry-decorating workshop.

itinerary.title: Tea & spice symphony
itinerary.body: Final intensive cooking session in the morning. Afternoon among tea plantations and on a working spice farm.

itinerary.title: Farewell
itinerary.body: A final traditional breakfast.

PACKAGES.notes: All-inclusive. Eco-friendly AC garden bungalow, 4 hands-on sessions, all meals, all excursions, unlimited bottled water.

PACKAGES.title: Kerala Cooking Adventure
PACKAGES.subtitle: 6 nights · 7 days · the long, layered one
PACKAGES.blurb: A full week. Six recipes per session, tea plantations, river days, our bean-to-bar workshop, and afternoons you won’t want to leave.
PACKAGES.longBlurb: Six nights gives us room to teach properly. You’ll cook a minimum of six recipes in each two-hour session, see tea country and the lorry-artists, ride the Muvattupuzha, visit a working farmers’ market, and spend an afternoon learning how chocolate is made from the bean up.
highlights: Choose demonstration or hands-on per session
Tea plantation, lorry-decorating workshop, local food industry

itinerary.title: Welcome
itinerary.body: Arrival, light lunch, history of Kerala cuisine, first cooking session, dinner.

itinerary.title: Markets & river
itinerary.body: Local markets, places of worship, river cruise. Two-hour cooking session, dinner showcasing your work.

itinerary.title: Farmers’ market
itinerary.body: Farmers’ market visit, then back to the kitchen for more masterpieces.

itinerary.title: Tea country
itinerary.body: Day trip into the tea country, evening at the long table.

itinerary.title: Chocolate workshop
itinerary.body: Bean-to-bar process. Afternoon cooking session.

itinerary.title: Lorry art & spice farms
itinerary.body: Lorry-decorating workshop, then a spice-farm tour. Farewell dinner.

itinerary.title: Farewell
itinerary.body: Final breakfast and onward.

PACKAGES.notes: Pricing on request — we tailor the program to your group size and preferences.

PACKAGES.title: Culinary Splendour in Kerala
PACKAGES.subtitle: 10 nights · 11 days · the masterclass
PACKAGES.blurb: Our deepest program. South Indian and North Indian vegetarian, Ayurvedic principles, the Western Ghats, the backwaters, and 10 nights to absorb it.
PACKAGES.longBlurb: Eleven days is enough time to actually change the way you cook. Mornings are for the science of spice. Afternoons go to dosa, idli, dal, paneer, breads. Days off go to tea, cardamom, the backwaters, and Ayurvedic cooking principles. You leave with technique, not recipes.
highlights: Both South Indian and North Indian vegetarian repertoires
Tea, cardamom and spice plantations in the Western Ghats
Signature backwaters cruise with a traditional lunch
Kerala Thali, paneer specialties, dal, regional breads, dosa, idli

itinerary.title: Foundations
itinerary.body: Orientation, the spice world, South Indian breakfast staples (dosa, idli), essential spice blends.

itinerary.title: Markets & deep dives
itinerary.body: Local markets, complex vegetable preparations, traditional rices, the Kerala Thali. Bean-to-bar workshop.

itinerary.title: Western Ghats & North India
itinerary.body: Tea and spice plantations. Paneer specialties, dals, regional breads.

itinerary.title: Backwater grandeur
itinerary.body: Scenic day trip to the backwaters for a private boat cruise and farewell dinner.

itinerary.title: Farewell
itinerary.body: Final breakfast and onward, with a small certificate.

PACKAGES.notes: All-inclusive AC accommodation, all meals, all cooking sessions, scheduled excursions.
PACKAGES.bestTime: February–May and July–September are the most vibrant.

PACKAGES.title: Taste of Kerala
PACKAGES.subtitle: 2 nights · 3 days · a short cultural sampler
PACKAGES.blurb: A long weekend in the midlands. The pineapple market, an ancient church, the lorry-painters, and our bungalows for two quiet nights.
PACKAGES.longBlurb: If you’re short on time but want more than sightseeing, this is our gentlest introduction. Two nights at The Pimenta, the village tour we send everyone on, an ancient church, the pineapple market, and the lorry-art tradition that has come to define the midlands for many of our guests.

itinerary.title: Arrival
itinerary.body: Settle into your bungalow. Refreshing lunch. The Midland Surprises Tour through the surrounding landscape.

itinerary.title: Discoveries
itinerary.body: Breakfast. Local market. Pineapple market and ancient church. The lorry-art tradition.

itinerary.title: Farewell
itinerary.body: Hearty breakfast and onward.

PACKAGES.notes: Includes 2 nights, breakfast/light lunch/dinner for 2 days, all itinerary trips, filtered water.

PACKAGES.title: Kerala Feel
PACKAGES.subtitle: 3 nights · 4 days · nature, culture, cuisine
PACKAGES.blurb: A tea plantation, an ancient church, a temple, the river ferry, the pineapple market — and a cooking class on the third afternoon if you want one.
PACKAGES.longBlurb: Three nights of slow looking. Tea plantation in the morning, river ferry after lunch, lorry-painting factory in between. The third afternoon is yours — take a cooking class, take an Ayurvedic massage, or take a nap.
highlights: Ancient Syrian Christian church and Hindu temples
River ride, pineapple market, terracotta artisans

itinerary.title: Arrival & garden
itinerary.body: Light lunch. Walk through the midlands and aromatic plantations. Dinner showcasing Kerala flavours.

itinerary.title: Tea trails
itinerary.body: Visit a verdant tea plantation. Charming village walk. Lorry-painting factory.

itinerary.title: Heritage & kitchen
itinerary.body: Ancient church and Hindu temples. River ride, pineapple market, terracotta artisans. Afternoon cooking class or Ayurvedic massage.

itinerary.title: Farewell
itinerary.body: Morning meal before departure.

PACKAGES.notes: Includes accommodation, daily meals, all transport in itinerary, one cooking class, filtered water.

PACKAGES.title: Discover the Heart of Kerala
PACKAGES.subtitle: 4 nights · 5 days · balanced cultural week
PACKAGES.blurb: Tea trails, sacred sites, terracotta artisans, the backwaters — four nights at the long table and one optional cooking session.
PACKAGES.longBlurb: Our most popular cultural week. The balance is what people love — a tea plantation, an ancient Syrian Christian church and a Hindu temple, a 3-hour backwater cruise with a packed picnic, terracotta artisans, the pineapple market, and one cooking session if you want one.
highlights: Syrian Christian Church and Hindu temple
Three-hour backwater cruise with packed picnic
Terracotta artisans, river ride, pineapple market
Optional Ayurvedic massage and cooking class

itinerary.title: Arrival & midlands
itinerary.body: Spice Garden Bungalow check-in. Light lunch. Guided walking tour of the midlands. Dinner at The Pimenta.

itinerary.title: Tea trails
itinerary.body: Scenic drive to a tea plantation. Walk the gardens. Afternoon: shopping in town, or interactive vegetarian cooking session.

itinerary.title: Sacred & artisanal
itinerary.body: Syrian Christian Church and Hindu temple. Light lunch, village walk. Terracotta artisans. River ride, pineapple market. Optional Ayurvedic massage.

itinerary.title: Backwaters
itinerary.body: Breakfast with local produce. Countryside drive to the backwaters. 3-hour cruise with packed picnic lunch. Evening dinner.

itinerary.title: Farewell
itinerary.body: Breakfast before departure.

PACKAGES.notes: Includes 4 nights, daily meals, all listed transport, filtered water. Tips and Ayurveda treatments excluded.

PACKAGES.title: Monsoon Wellness Holiday
PACKAGES.subtitle: Tailor-made · 5 to 21 nights · June to October
PACKAGES.blurb: Yoga, Ayurvedic cooking, partner Ayurvedic massages at nearby centres, and the long monsoon afternoons that make Kerala famous.
PACKAGES.longBlurb: Between June and October the rains come and the farm changes character. Mootil pazham fruits everywhere, the pepper greens up, the air is washed clean. This is the right time for an Ayurvedic week with us — yoga in the mornings, cooking lessons focused on dosha balance, treatments at the trusted Ayurvedic centres we’ve worked with for thirty years, and afternoons on the veranda watching the rain.
highlights: Ayurvedic massages at nearby partner centres
Library of Ayurveda, yoga and healthy-living books

itinerary.title: Yoga & teaching
itinerary.body: A gentle yoga session, followed by Ayurvedic cooking guidance — understanding doshas, balancing meals, the rasas.

itinerary.title: Treatments
itinerary.body: Customised Ayurvedic massages and therapies at a partner centre, ten minutes away. Or rest, read, walk.

itinerary.title: Long table
itinerary.body: Ayurvedically informed dinner, conversation, the library, the rain.

PACKAGES.notes: Tailored entirely to your needs and length of stay. June 1 to October 31 only.

PACKAGES.title: Tailor-Made Holiday
PACKAGES.subtitle: We build it around you
PACKAGES.blurb: Tell us what you love to eat and what you want to see. We’ll build the rest.
PACKAGES.longBlurb: Some guests arrive with very specific things in mind — a son who loves chocolate, a mother who wants Ayurveda, a partner who only wants temples, three days extra to do nothing at all. We’re used to it. Write to us with what you’d love, and we’ll write back with a plan and a price.
highlights: Any combination of cooking, culture, wellness, nothing-at-all
NRI homecoming programs (kids meeting amma’s Kerala for the first time)

itinerary.title: Tell us
itinerary.body: Write with dates, group, dietary needs, and what you’d like the trip to feel like.

itinerary.title: We sketch
itinerary.body: Within a day, a proposed program and a price.

itinerary.title: We refine
itinerary.body: One or two rounds of conversation. Then a 25% deposit confirms.

PACKAGES.notes: We answer every enquiry personally, usually within a day.

ROOMS.name: Bungalow No. 1 — The Pepper
ROOMS.blurb: High ceilings, exposed brick, twin beds, a private veranda over the pepper vines.

ROOMS.name: Bungalow No. 2 — The Cardamom
ROOMS.blurb: Tucked behind the spice grove. Writing desk, soft towels, the morning birds.

ROOMS.name: Bungalow No. 3 — The Cocoa
ROOMS.blurb: Closest to the kitchen. Best choice for early cooking-program starts.

ROOMS.name: Bungalow No. 4 — The Nutmeg
ROOMS.blurb: The quiet one. Deepest into the trees. Hammock on the veranda.

ROOMS.name: Main-house rooms (budget / long stay)
ROOMS.blurb: First-floor rooms in the main house, with a shared veranda. For budget travellers and long stays.

STAY_MODES.title: Bed & Breakfast
STAY_MODES.blurb: Just the bungalow and a generous breakfast. Days yours to wander. Two-night minimum.
STAY_MODES.notes: We’ll recommend nearby restaurants for lunch and dinner.

STAY_MODES.title: Stay + All Meals (Full Board)
STAY_MODES.blurb: All three meals at the long kitchen table. Thoughtfully prepared, tailored to your diet. Bespoke pricing.
STAY_MODES.notes: Pricing tailored to dietary preference (vegan, traditional, Ayurvedic prep/recovery, etc).

STAY_MODES.title: Whole Property
STAY_MODES.blurb: All four bungalows, the long kitchen, the family’s time, and the 6.5 acres. Three-night minimum.
STAY_MODES.notes: Up to 8 guests. For families, writing retreats, small celebrations.

items.title: Old Syrian Christian churches
items.body: Some of Kerala’s oldest parishes — the St Thomas tradition.

items.title: Hindu temples and festivals
items.body: Including the arattu when the river floods up to the temple.

items.title: Lorry-decorating workshop
items.body: How a Kerala lorry gets its face. A favourite afternoon.

items.title: Rubber plantation & rubber-band factory
items.body: The least-touristed hour any of our guests have.

items.title: Artisan chocolate (bean-to-bar)
items.body: A neighbour, 40 minutes away, who makes some of South India’s best.

items.title: Backwater cruise
items.body: Three hours on the kind of boat the locals use, picnic lunch.

items.title: Muvattupuzha river ferry
items.body: The local commuter ferry, not the houseboat circus.

items.title: Ayurvedic treatments
items.body: At trusted partner centres ten minutes away.

items.title: Tea plantations
items.body: A drive into the Western Ghats, a walk among the rows.

items.title: Clay-pot and terracotta
items.body: Visits, and an optional one-day pottery workshop.

items.title: Local herbs & spice shop
items.body: Quality local product, packed to international standards.

items.title: Thodupuzha & Muvattupuzha towns
items.body: Snack-making, tailors, fabric, shoes — the things tourists never see.

REVIEWS.name: Jaina S.
REVIEWS.short: Extended my 2 nights to 3 without a second thought.
REVIEWS.long: Jacob made this experience fantastic — from the amazing cooking classes, to the guided tours around the area and taking me out for local delicacies.

REVIEWS.name: Sally F.
REVIEWS.short: The day trips give you a slice of Kerala life.
REVIEWS.long: Jacob is a super sweet and attentive host who really tailors the program around the food you want to learn how to cook.

REVIEWS.name: Jamie H.
REVIEWS.short: Don’t think twice — this is the real Kerala.
REVIEWS.long: Jacob is a phenomenal host, chef, teacher, neighbourhood guide. A truly caring individual is behind The Pimenta.

REVIEWS.name: Brian R.
REVIEWS.long: I enjoyed the combination of morning markets, trying different restaurants for lunch and evening cooking. Jacob sources everything locally.

REVIEWS.name: Corinne C.
REVIEWS.long: The bungalows are surrounded by beautiful nature. We would have loved to stay longer and cook more with Jacob.

FAQS.a: We sit on 6.5 acres of spice gardens in Kadalikad, between Thodupuzha and Muvattupuzha in Kerala’s midlands. About 90 minutes from Kochi International Airport.
Easiest: a driver from Kochi (we’ll arrange one). Trains to Aluva, Ernakulam, or Kottayam also work — we’ll pick you up from either.
Yes. Everything we cook and teach is vegetarian or vegan. It’s how we eat, it’s what we know best, and it’s what Kerala’s traditional plate looks like.
Very. Madhu handles the prep so you focus on the craft — the tempering, the layering, the tasting. You’ll cook 4–6 dishes a day on most programs.

FAQS.q: I have dietary restrictions. Is that OK?
FAQS.a: Yes. Vegan, gluten-free, no-nuts, Jain — just tell us when you enquire. Our whole kitchen is already vegetarian.

FAQS.q: What’s the best time to visit?
FAQS.a: October to March is dry and pleasant. June to October is monsoon — dramatic, green, and the right time for our Ayurvedic Wellness program. February–May and July–September are when the cooking programs feel most alive.

FAQS.q: Can I stay without doing a cooking program?
FAQS.a: Of course — our Bed & Breakfast and Stay + All Meals options are for guests who want only the farm, the meals, and the quiet.
We do. Not great wifi, but enough wifi.

FAQS.q: Can we rent the whole property?
FAQS.a: Yes — all four bungalows, up to 8 guests. Popular with families, writing retreats, and small celebrations. Three-night minimum.
Since the early 1990s we’ve been chemical-free. The farm runs partly on solar (including hot water), and we employ locally. Greenery, space, and silence are the only luxuries we promise.
All ages welcome on Tailor-Made and Whole Property stays. Multi-night cooking residencies work best for ages 14 and up.

ABOUT.title: Rediscovering responsible tourism and culinary retreats with our story
ABOUT.intro: Welcome to Haritha Farms, a sanctuary of sustainable tourism and culinary retreats in the heart of Kerala's midlands.

sections.title: Haritha Farms
sections.body: Established in 1962 by the Mathew family, Haritha Farms began as a rubber plantation and grew into a living enclave for green living. The family turned away from pesticides and chemical fertilizers, choosing a forest garden rich with crops and medicinal plants instead.

sections.title: Pioneers of eco-agri-tourism
sections.body: In 1992, the Mathew family opened their doors to day visitors and became early pioneers of eco-agri-tourism in southern India. Over time, the stay expanded to include The Pimenta, a cluster of independent bungalows named after the Portuguese word for black pepper.

sections.title: The Pimenta experience
sections.body: Set in the foothills of the Western Ghats, The Pimenta offers four bungalows facing a verdant tropical garden, a communal dining area, and a kitchen where guests can get an inside look at authentic Indian cooking.

sections.title: Comfortable and intimate
sections.body: Guests can slow down on private verandas or gather in the bohemian-style shared spaces. We keep the setting intimate, comfortable, and rooted in the rhythms of a real Kerala homestead, with practical comforts including Wi-Fi across the property.

sections.title: Culinary and cultural immersion
sections.body: Jacob Mathew has hosted and shaped experiences here for over 25 years. Alongside healthy South Indian vegetarian cuisine and hands-on cookery courses, stays can include tea plantations, local businesses, boat trips on the Muvattupuzha River, markets, and places of worship across Kerala's cultural landscape.

ABOUT.teamBody: Our team includes Jacob Mathew, Mrs. Mathew, Madhu Shankar, Ranjeet Mathew Jacob, and Ancy P. Anto in the back office. Together they create the feeling of being welcomed by a Keralite family rather than processed by a hotel.
ABOUT.ctaBody: If you seek a thoughtful blend of comfort, sustainability, culture, and cuisine, The Pimenta at Haritha Farms is ready to help you plan a more personal Kerala stay.

TERMS.title: Terms and Conditions
TERMS.intro: The following terms and conditions apply to all bookings made with The Pimenta Homestay and form the contract between the host and the guest. By making a booking, you confirm that you have read the website and the details of the program you are booking, understand the risks associated with it, and have the authority to accept these conditions on behalf of yourself and your party. The contract is established once we accept any payment towards the booking or issue our confirmation or invoice, whichever happens first.
TERMS.acknowledgement: It is assumed that you have read the terms and conditions and the details of each program before booking. By completing a booking, you confirm that you understand and accept everything stated here. If you have any questions or concerns, please contact us before confirming your booking.

sections.title: General booking terms
sections.body: All bookings are subject to these terms and conditions. By booking with The Pimenta Homestay, you agree to the terms outlined here.

sections.title: Experience packages and culinary retreats
sections.body: When booking our experience packages or culinary retreats, a non-refundable deposit of 30% of the total booking value is required at the time of booking. This deposit will only be refunded if the booking is not accepted by us for any reason or if, due to unforeseen circumstances, we are unable to provide the course you booked. Once availability is confirmed, payment details will be shared and immediate payment is required. A proper receipt will be issued after payment is received. The remaining balance is payable upon arrival.

sections.title: Stay bookings
sections.body: For bookings involving a stay or a stay with food, a deposit equal to one night's accommodation must be paid in advance. If you fail to arrive on the booked day, the deposit will be forfeited. If, for any reason, we are unable to provide accommodation on the booked day, the booking amount will be refunded. For multi-day stays, you must confirm on arrival and make payment for the remaining days to secure the rest of the booking.

sections.title: International guest requirements
sections.body: Foreign travellers must provide a copy of the front page of their passport, along with a contact phone number, mailing address, and emergency contact details. You are responsible for holding a valid visa on arrival and for the full duration of your stay. If you do not hold a valid visa, accommodation will be denied.

sections.title: Indian guest requirements
sections.body: Indian guests must provide a valid photo identification card for all occupants on arrival, along with a valid address and contact phone number. A working contact number and emergency contact person are required.

sections.title: Travel insurance
sections.body: It is a condition of booking that you arrange adequate holiday insurance covering medical expenses, personal baggage, personal accidents, loss or damage to property, cancellation or curtailment, personal liability, legal expenses, and emergencies.

sections.title: Governing law
sections.body: The contract, including these terms and conditions, is governed by the laws of India.
